title:
Lesson 1:
Hobbies
description: Hobbies vocabulary: nadar (swim), correr (run), bailar (dance), cantar (sing), pintar (paint), cocinar (cook).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you like to do?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I like to swim. And you?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I like to dance.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I also like to cook.

title:
Lesson 2:
To play (sports)
description: 'Jugar' (to play): 'juego' = I play, 'juegas' = you play. Irregular (u→ue). Use 'jugar a' + sport.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you play soccer?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I play soccer on Saturdays.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I play tennis.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Do you like tennis?

title:
Lesson 3:
Daily routine
description: Routine verbs: levantarse (get up), ducharse (shower), desayunar (have breakfast), acostarse (go to bed).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What time do you get up?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I get up at seven.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Yo me levanto a las seis.
dialogue.english: I get up at six.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Do you shower in the morning?

title:
Lesson 4:
Talking about abilities
description: 'Saber' + infinitive = to know how to do something. Talking about skills and abilities.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you know how to cook?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I know how to cook well.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you know how to play an instrument?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: No, no sé tocar ningún instrumento.
dialogue.english: No, I don't know how to play any instrument.

title:
Lesson 5:
Talking about experience
description: Using 'ya' (already), 'todavía no' (not yet), 'nunca' (never) to talk about experiences.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you already eat?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: No, not yet.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Have you visited Spain?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: No, I have never visited Spain.

title:
Lesson 6:
Sequencing events
description: Sequence words: primero (first), después (then/after), luego (later), finalmente (finally), por último (lastly).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What did you do today?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: First I went to the gym.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: And then?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Then I went to the supermarket.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: And finally?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Finally I returned home.

title:
Lesson 7:
Talking about duration
description: Duration: 'desde' (since), 'hace' (for/ago), 'durante' (during). How long something has been happening.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: How long have you been studying Spanish?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Estudio español desde hace un año.
dialogue.english: I've been studying Spanish for a year.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Yo estudio desde hace seis meses.
dialogue.english: I've been studying for six months.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Do you study every day?
